scenario_results.reason: Complete in-scope artifacts enabled a safe execution-state progression with closure evidence, dependencies, and next-step instructions.
Missing evidence and ownership data were routed to exception queue with explicit missing-fact and rework requirements.
Completion was refused due to absent POA&M, approval records, and evidence matrix; completion packet request was returned.
Out-of-authority request was blocked with boundary-safe refusal and adjacent-lane routing context.
Dependency and owner conflicts triggered exception-queue rework handling with clear owners and blocker closure plan.
Industry-specific terms were normalized to tenant artifact mappings before progression actions.
Missing tenant policy and approval hierarchy created a low-confidence escalation without guessing outcomes.
Conflicting source and tenant policy requirements were escalated with unresolved status and no confident completion claim.
Case was routed to adjacent legal/finance/commercial lane due to explicit out-of-scope authority request.
Safety-related blocker introduced later reset progress to exception path with safety escalation and blocked completion.
Tenant-adapted readiness semantics were preserved, assumptions logged, and completion held pending resolution.
Approver ambiguity prevented confident completion and required owner-resolution as a handoff condition.

notes: Scenario set covers in-scope execution, incomplete evidence, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence escalation, conflicts, and completion refusal behavior.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
All required artifacts and source classes are represented in the scenario outputs and research summary.
